sections.question_synthetic: Whether two Timber Cove Water District Board members have a conflict of interest under Regulation 18702.5(a)(2) when voting on drilling exploratory water wells, two of which are located on their properties, and whether the "public generally" exception applies given that the wells would benefit a substantial portion of the district population.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document lacks formal Q/A structure. Contains property ownership data table. Written as informal inquiry requesting FPPC staff review and telephone discussion. No formal FPPC conclusion or advice is provided in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether two Timber Cove Water District Board members have a conflict of interest under Regulation 18702.5(a)(2) when voting on drilling exploratory water wells, two of which are located on their properties, and whether the "public generally" exception applies given that the wells would benefit a substantial portion of the district population.

embedding.summary: A law firm representing Timber Cove Water District seeks FPPC guidance on whether two Board members who own multiple properties in the district have a conflict of interest in voting on proposed exploratory water well drilling, particularly where two well sites are on their properties.
